title: cache and docker-archive problem
body:
### Issue Description

When using `buildah build --layers --cache-to registry.com --cache-from registry.com --tag docker-archive:./image.tar` I get this error 

> Error: locating new copy of image "773fb5edcc74a0838b9c5daa01c7555e3a5670679db589f068566a2dedcbe9bf" (i.e., "docker-archive:/builds/image.tar"): trying to resolve a non-containers-storage: reference "docker-archive:/builds/image.tar"

when not using layers this is working. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.build a image with layers and using the docker-archive to output image as tar.


### Describe the results you received

> Error: locating new copy of image "773fb5edcc74a0838b9c5daa01c7555e3a5670679db589f068566a2dedcbe9bf" (i.e., "docker-archive:/builds/image.tar"): trying to resolve a non-containers-storage: reference "docker-archive:/builds/image.tar"


### Describe the results you expected

Cached Layers and a docker-archive `.tar` file that contains valid container image so it can be scanned by 

When using other methods like `--output` i get this Problem. 
https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy/issues/1080

### buildah version output

```yaml
quay.io/buildah/stable:v1.40.1
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run `buildah info` for any reason, please provide the operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
-none
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

gitlab docker runner 

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
